response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack/web engineer with broad experience across TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, but the profile fundamentally mismatches this role. The position requires native-level Japanese, strong C for UNIX development, Linux kernel source-code analysis and modification, and deep embedded networking security expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 switching) — none of which are evidenced. His only substantial C/C++ work was a six-month stint at National Instruments over a decade ago, and there is no networking, security, or firmware background.
strengths: Long, consistent software engineering career with senior/staff-level roles
Some systems-level exposure: C/C++ at National Instruments, low-latency DLang services, Linux proficiency
Strong engineering practices (TDD, CI/CD) and open-source contribution track record
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency — a hard requirement (native level)
No embedded firmware, Linux kernel, or network-switch development experience
No demonstrated security-technology experience (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption, tamper detection)

key_factors.factor: Solid general engineering seniority and Linux background
